title: Ultra Japanese Snow Peas Tonkotsu Ramen
description: A sour, snappy crowd‑pleaser ramen featuring snow peas and a rich pork bone broth.
instructions: Rinse pork belly bones and place them in a large pot with 4 liters of cold water, bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 1.5 hours to extract the broth.
Skim off any foam that rises to the surface during simmering.
After simmering, strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot and discard the solids.
Add 1 tablespoon of salt, 3 tablespoons of soy sauce, 2 tablespoons of mirin, 2 tablespoons of sake, and 1 tablespoon of rice vinegar to the strained broth, stirring until dissolved.
Bring the seasoned broth to a gentle simmer and let it cook for an additional 30 minutes to meld the flavors.
While the broth simmers, boil the ramen noodles in a separate pot of salted water according to package instructions, then drain and set aside.
Trim the fresh ginger into thin julienne strips and quickly blanch them in the boiling broth for 30 seconds to release aroma.
Toss the snow peas and sliced shiitake mushrooms into the simmering broth for the last 3 minutes, then remove them with a slotted spoon.
Divide the cooked noodles among four bowls, ladle the hot broth over them, then top each bowl with blanched ginger, sautéed snow peas, mushrooms, and a soft‑boiled egg half‑sliced.
Serve immediately, garnishing with optional sesame seeds or a drizzle of chili oil if desired.
